title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones, Jr. will not seek reelection, ending his 32-year tenure as a Massachusetts state representative.
Jones, 61, has served as the Republican Minority Leader since 2002 and represented the 20th Middlesex District.
Elected 17 times since 1994, Jones has participated in over 8,180 roll call votes during his legislative career.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents were deployed to Logan Airport, despite President Trump's order to send agents to airports.
ICE agents were sent to 14 busy airports to address delays and enforce immigration policies, CNN reported.
Logan Airport remains unaffected by delays impacting other major airports amid the ongoing government shutdown.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave as of February 26 pending an external investigation.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is serving as acting chief of the Fitchburg Fire Department.
The city of Fitchburg declined to comment further on the investigation, citing it as a personnel matter.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was stabbed in the abdomen and leg outside 72 Seaport Blvd in Boston.
The stabbing occurred around 2:44 a.m. on Sunday; the victim's current condition is unknown.
Police have not identified any suspects and have made no arrests; the investigation is ongoing.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's bench trial began in Essex County Superior Court for assault with a dangerous weapon.
Fitzsimmons, a police officer, allegedly aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, prompting him to shoot her.
Defense claims Fitzsimmons intended suicide, not harm to others; trial overseen by Judge Jeffrey Karp continues Tuesday.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley hockey team, celebrating their Rhode Island Division II state championship victory.
The Blackstone Valley team met Bruins players, attended practice, and took photos at Warrior Ice Arena in Brighton.
The visit followed a tragic shooting incident affecting Blackstone Valley's captain, with the community rallying in support.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson, who was released by the Giants earlier this month.
Hudson, a 6-foot-5 lineman, has played in 60 games with 19 starts in his NFL career.
Hudson will likely serve as backup at right tackle, adding depth behind starter Morgan Moses.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Boston Bruins prospect, hasn't signed a pro deal after ending sophomore season at Boston College.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to including young prospects in the lineup if they meet performance standards.
Hagens' potential entry into the NHL is complicated by contract negotiations and possible starting locations.

title: ESPN reporter explains why June 1st could be a big day for teams looking to trade for A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown trade offers could increase after June 1st due to reduced financial impact on the Eagles.
If A.J. Brown is traded after June 1st, the Eagles' dead cap hit drops from $43.5 million to $16.353 million.
The Patriots may offer their first-round pick for Brown, reflecting high trade value similar to a Quinnen Williams-type deal.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Allyson Bogdanski from Medford is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother's battle against breast cancer.
Bogdanski's mother, a patient at Dana-Farber, is a two-time breast cancer survivor receiving ongoing treatment there.
The marathon run is both a tribute to her mother's courage and a thank-you to Dana-Farber's care team.
